hooks.description: Classify session phase and domain, write context routing recommendation
Block bash commands that would expose secrets or credentials
Detect prompt injection patterns in file content being written
If the bash command was pytest, gate merge on test/QC verdict
Detect when code diverges from spec docs
Compile session memory to project knowledge base on session end
Sync Azure Board task statuses on session end
Clear session context state files on session end (/tmp works under Git Bash on Windows; Phase 4 port to Python will generalize)
Self-gated: auto-generate SRE runbook stub if notification text matches sre_alert/production_incident
Self-gated: trigger LLMOps alert triage if notification matches llmops_alert/token_cost/model_drift/hallucination
Self-gated: notify when architectural decisions are detected in notification text

_notes: Hook events use Claude Code's documented names: SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionEnd, Notification, Stop, SubagentStop, PreCompact, UserPromptSubmit.
Git pre-commit is wired into .git/hooks/pre-commit by project-init (not a Claude Code hook event).
skill-router runs on SessionStart — classifies phase/domain, writes the route file to the OS temp dir.
secrets-scanner runs PreToolUse:Bash — must read the tool_input.command from stdin JSON, NOT $BASH_COMMAND.
injection-detector runs PreToolUse:Edit|Write — inspects the file content being written, not a repo-wide scan.
post-test runs PostToolUse:Bash but must self-gate to only act when the executed command was pytest.
doc-drift runs PostToolUse:Edit|Write to catch spec divergence on every file edit.
mem-compile + board-sync + state cleanup run SessionEnd (not Stop — Stop fires per-turn).
Notification hooks self-gate on the notification message text since Claude Code does not regex-match Notification matchers.
